response.reasoning: Candidate has ~15 years of experience, far exceeding the 6+ year requirement. Current role at re:cinq explicitly involves full-stack TypeScript/Node.js work plus AI-agent infrastructure including MCP code generators — a direct hit on the 'AI agent development' requirement, which is often the hardest to find. Optilyz tenure covers TypeScript, AWS, React, MongoDB; RIDE covers Node/TS/GraphQL with TDD/CI/CD discipline. Strong, demonstrable testing culture (built Trial test runner and Fluent-Asserts in D, taught Selenium/TDD courses, ran Refactoring Dojos) — Vitest specifically isn't mentioned but the underlying competence is unambiguous. Gaps: Nest.js and Vitest not named explicitly; Kotlin not in the stack (Java is, lightly); a lot of historical depth is in DLang which isn't in the JD. Bachelor's in CS satisfies the degree requirement. Location (Lagos) and only basic German could matter depending on remote policy but aren't disqualifying. Overall a senior, polyglot, test-disciplined full-stack engineer with the rare AI-agent infra experience.
strengths: Direct AI-agent infrastructure experience (MCP generators, agentic frameworks) — rare and explicitly required
Strong TypeScript/Node/React/MongoDB/AWS track record at Optilyz and RIDE with TDD/CI/CD discipline
Deep testing expertise — built a test runner and assertion library, taught TDD and Selenium courses
concerns: Nest.js and Vitest not mentioned explicitly in resume
Kotlin absent and Java exposure is light/historical
Significant portion of career depth is in DLang, which isn't part of the target stack
response.justification: Bogdan clears the seniority bar comfortably and hits the two highest-signal requirements: production fullstack TypeScript/Node/React work and concrete AI-agent infrastructure experience. The missing Nest.js and Vitest specifics are low-risk gaps for an engineer with this much framework breadth and a documented testing pedigree; Kotlin is a softer concern but the JD lists it as secondary. Worth interviewing to validate Nest.js ramp-up speed and Kotlin appetite.
